name: Liechtenstein
avoid: Most museums in Vaduz are closed on Mondays
Evening bus frequencies thin out outside Vaduz, so check the LIEmobil timetable before late dinners
stopoverNote: Liechtenstein is tiny and is best covered in 2–3 days, often paired with eastern Switzerland or western Austria.

cities.name: Vaduz
cities.notes: Compact, walkable capital for museums, the Städtle promenade and Vaduz Castle exterior views

cities.name: Malbun
cities.notes: Liechtenstein's alpine resort for easy ridge walks in summer and family-friendly skiing in winter

cities.name: Triesenberg
cities.notes: Walser heritage village with valley viewpoints, traditional houses and a museum stop

cities.name: Balzers
cities.notes: Southern Liechtenstein base for Burg Gutenberg, vineyards and quiet Rhine-side walks

itinerary.sim: An Airalo Eurolink eSIM is the simplest short-stay option at roughly ₹450–₹1,900 depending on data; Swiss or Austrian roaming plans also work seamlessly in Liechtenstein.

Vaduz.name: Vaduz
Vaduz.note: The capital is extremely compact, so one well-paced day covers the main sights on foot plus one or two museums.

activities.name: Walk up for Vaduz Castle exterior views and panorama over the Rhine Valley
activities.tip: The castle is the private residence of the princely family, so only the exterior and viewpoints are open year-round.

activities.name: Kunstmuseum Liechtenstein
PostMuseum Vaduz (Postage Stamp Museum; admission via Liechtenstein National Museum ticket)
Liechtenstein National Museum
Städtle promenade, Cathedral of St. Florin and Red House exterior walk

Malbun.name: Malbun
Malbun.note: Use Malbun as the outdoors day: hike the ridge in summer or ski the compact slopes in winter, then head back to Vaduz or Triesenberg for the night.

days.theme: Alpine Liechtenstein: Ridge Walks or Ski Day

activities.name: Sareis chairlift round trip for easy alpine views
Malbun–Sareis panoramic walk or summer meadow trails
Malbun day ski pass in winter
Village walk past the chapel and mountain hotels
Family-friendly sledging or snow-play areas in winter

Triesenberg.name: Triesenberg
Triesenberg.note: Triesenberg works well as a half- to full-day cultural stop between Vaduz and Malbun, with good valley views and a simple Walser heritage circuit.

activities.name: Walser Museum Triesenberg
Känzile viewpoint over the Rhine Valley
St. Joseph parish church and old village lanes
Short village walk to traditional Walser wooden houses
LIEmobil bus ride up from Vaduz for panoramic switchback views

Balzers.name: Balzers
Balzers.note: Balzers is the optional fourth stop when you want a slower final day focused on Gutenberg Castle and the country’s quieter southern end.

activities.name: Burg Gutenberg outer courtyard and hilltop approach
Castle chapel and rose garden (May–October Sundays)
Balzers old village lanes and parish church walk
Rhine embankment walk or cycle on the valley floor
Advance-booked Gutenberg Castle guided tour
activities.cost: from ₹4,750/person equivalent if 20-share a CHF 100 group tour

extras: Liechtenstein has no airport; the easiest approach is Zurich Airport to Sargans or Buchs by train, then LIEmobil into Vaduz.
The country uses Swiss francs, and a simple working conversion is CHF 1 ≈ ₹95 for quick budgeting.
Vaduz is fully walkable, while Triesenberg, Malbun and Balzers are easiest by frequent LIEmobil buses rather than car rental.
Kunstmuseum Liechtenstein is free on Wednesdays, while most Vaduz museums close on Mondays.
If you only have 2 days, focus on Vaduz plus either Malbun for outdoors or Triesenberg for Walser culture.

Vaduz: Vaduz Städtle Cathedral of St. Florin
